# Transcrição Completa: How to Increase Show Rate
[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "9 How to Increase Show Rate"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Título com ícones de calendário e presença em chamadas
[00:02] Okay. So, whether you have book call funnels, whether you're doing outbound dialing, whether you're dealing with inbound lead forms that are then scheduling follow-up calls, you're going to have to know how to keep your show rate high.
[00:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Common Sales Team Complaint"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Vendedores frustrados com no-shows
[00:20] So, the most interesting feedback I ever get from sales teams is they just contact me as the marketer and they say, "Ah, man, dude, like people are booking in, but they're just not showing up." Or, "Oh, I'm reaching out to people. They're scheduling calls, but then they're not showing up."
[00:35] I then ask them, I'm like, "Well, what are you doing to get them to show up?" and they say, "Ah, well, you know, sometimes I'll text and I'll confirm with them." That's about it, you know, and there's a huge problem there.
[00:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Below Average Organization Approach"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Automação básica: emails e textos de confirmação
[00:55] Besides marketing automation, here's how a typical and I would say this is a below average organization deals with show rates. The typical show rate is dealt with by automation, which is going to be emails and texts.
[01:10] So emails, text messages, just simply confirming the appointment time, those basic reminders, the calendarly schedule ones, you know, just regular marketing automation will do.
[01:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Decent Organization Addition"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Automação + follow-up manual básico
[01:30] And in addition to that, they'll get the decent ones, the decent typical sales organizations, not the ones that are below average. They'll do a manual follow-up as well with the salesperson. And all that means is it's typically just a basic like, "Hey, just wanted to make sure you're actually going to show up."
[01:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Above Average Organization Strategy"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Automação + Follow-up manual + SELL THE CALL
[01:55] Now what an above average sales organization does is quite different. The above average sales organization does not just rely on marketing automation. They do it, but they don't just rely on that. They also will do that manual follow-up attempt.
[02:15] But in addition, when the call is being scheduled, they will sell the call. So, what do I mean by that?
[02:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Selling The Call Concept"
**NÚMEROS:** 9:00 AM example
**VISUAL:** Processo de venda da própria chamada
[02:30] Remember what you're essentially asking when you're saying, "Hey, I'd love to get on a follow-up call with you. Do you have time at this time?" "Yes, I do. I'll make sure to show up." Great.
[02:45] The great sales organizations, the one that are above average, what I mean is they sell the call. They tell the person when they're actually booking, "Hey, I think it's going to be awesome when you show up for this call at 9:00 a.m. tomorrow."
[03:00] "Just real quick, I want to make sure that we go through this. It's important you understand the reason we do this call is because," and then we want to explain ourselves. We want to go through a process that actually sells the person on the fact that it's worth their time showing up.
[03:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Bad Incentives vs Good Incentives"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Ebook riscado vs incentivos reais que prospects querem
[03:25] We can do this a few different ways. Some clients that we've worked with in the past, they'll create incentives that the person actually wants. Let me tell you some dumbass incentives. A random ebook that you think is going to be valuable for the person. A lot of them aren't.
[03:45] And a lot of people don't want to spend that extra time to read through it. Some people will say, "Hey, if you show up for the call, we're going to give you an ebook." Terrible idea.
[04:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Effective Incentive Script"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Script de incentivo para action takers
[04:05] You want to find things that they actually want. "Hey, potential lead, I think it's awesome that you want to show up at 9:00 a.m. tomorrow, and I'm happy to book that in. Just real quick, I want to make sure we're on the same page."
[04:20] "If you show up to this call tomorrow, we incentivize action takers. We incentivize clients and customers who actually do what they're going to say. If you show up tomorrow and we end up doing a deal a couple weeks from now, we're going to give you X to sweeten the deal up a bit."
[04:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Fast Action Taker Incentives"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Escala de incentivos baseada na velocidade de ação
[04:45] "Just show up for the call tomorrow. Like I said, we don't even need to close it tomorrow. If we do close it tomorrow, by the way, we have this incentive for the fast action takers. But hey, if it takes a couple days, that's fine. We have incentives to get you to do a deal with us that are better and better the faster you do the deal."
[05:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Types of Incentives"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Desconto, produto adicional, software, onboarding extra
[05:10] Could be a discount on the deal. Could be an added product to the deal. It could be just an additional onboarding meeting or some shit. It could be a software that you were already going to sell them anyway in your main offer.
[05:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "5-Part Offer Example"
**NÚMEROS:** 5 parts + 6th bonus part
**VISUAL:** Oferta com 5 componentes principais + 6º como bônus
[05:30] So, let me give you an example of this. If I have an offer that has, let's say, five things in it, okay? It's a five-part offer. This might be a bonus already. They might not even know that the fifth part of the offer exists yet.
[05:45] They likely know what you sell, but they likely don't know the details of what comes with what you sell. So, what I mean by that is under the guise that they don't know everything, I can take something that would already come with it anyway and make it appear as though I'm going to give it to them if they do something sooner.
[06:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Explaining The Call Process"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Estrutura da chamada: apresentação, perguntas, fit assessment
[06:10] We also just want to make sure that the person gets generally what's going to happen on the call. "Hey client, really appreciate you want to do the call tomorrow at 9:00 a.m. We want to make sure you understand why we do this call."
[06:25] "So what we typically do on this is we show you a little bit about a presentation, how everything works, how the investment details are. We kind of go through some questions that you're likely going to have. We also ask you some questions to make sure that it's a good fit."
[06:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Getting Verbal Commitment"
**NÚMEROS:** 50% no-show rate
**VISUAL:** Pessoa fazendo compromisso verbal
[06:45] "If you don't mind, I mean, we could just knock it out now if you have time and you could potentially do the call right then and there. But if you're going to do the call tomorrow still..."
[07:00] "Is that something that you want to do tomorrow, or would you prefer that we knock it out right now?" Most people are just going to say, "Yeah, I'm fine to do it tomorrow." Great.
[07:10] "In that case, I want to just get a verbal commitment from you. Surprisingly, about half the people who say they're going to show up tomorrow don't. That's why we typically try to have the conversation right now instead of waiting until tomorrow."
[07:25] "If you are going to do it tomorrow, I just want to get a verbal commitment from you. Are you actually going to show up? Can I get your word?"
[07:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Selfie Video Follow-up"
**NÚMEROS:** None
**VISUAL:** Pessoa gravando selfie video de confirmação
[07:40] We want to get the person to commit. People don't like to let themselves down. If you get somebody to commit to something, sure, don't get me wrong, they're potentially lying to you, and I acknowledge that, guys, but there's going to be a majority of people that when they commit actually end up showing up.
[07:55] The other things that we could do, I taught you about the selfie video in past communications. I want to be clear, we want to do the selfie videos, okay? Selfie videos instead of just the generic manual follow-up work a lot better for the let down bias.
[08:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
**TEXTO:** "Combined Tactics Results"
**NÚMEROS:** 20-30% increase in show rates
**VISUAL:** Gráfico mostrando aumento combinado de show rate
[08:20] We want to leverage the data that we have. We want to sell the call. We want to do selfie videos. We want to rely on the marketing automation. We can still do the manual follow-up as a secondary after the selfie video.
[08:30] But overall, once again, these tactics typically increase show rates anywhere from 20 to 30% on average. There is no magic pill. There is no one thing I want to be very clear that increases it by itself 20 to 30%. It's really a combination of all of these that does it. See you in the next video.
**🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy ANIQUILA a ingenuidade de vendedores que esperam que prospects apareçam por mágica! A diferença entre organizações medíocres e superiores é BRUTAL - vender a própria chamada é GENIAL. Jeremy destrói incentivos idiotas como ebooks e revela como transformar partes da oferta em bônus irresistíveis. O compromisso verbal é psicologia pura - pessoas odeiam decepcionar a si mesmas. Esta é estratégia de DOMINAÇÃO total do show rate!**
